Re: Request for Advice: Government Code S 83114(b)
Dear Ms. Donovan:
The purpose of this letter is to request advice on behalf of
CarI springei, a member of the City of DubLin Planning Commission.
Mr. Springer is also a traffic 
engineer by profession and,
"o.r".,ting November L, l-990, wiII be employed by TJKM, a traffic
engineeriig firm that serves as traffic 
enqineer for the City of
ou6tin pursuant to contract. The undersigned is the City Attorney
of the City of Dublin.
OUESTION
Does Mr. Springer have a financial interest in decisions made
by him as a Planning Commission member where his employer, TJKM,
n-as prepared a traffic analysis or some other report for the City
of OuUfln requiring Mr. Springer to disclose such interest and to
disqualify himself?
ANALYSIS
As traffic 
engineer for the City, TJKM (a) prepares traffic
analyses for City staff's use in recommending the impositi.on of
trafiic 
impact fees on applicants, (b) prepares traffic 
analyses
for use in preparation of environmental documents (EfRs, negative
declarations) on applications for development approvals and on
general plan amend.ments, and (c) otherwise responds to planning
staff j-nquiries regarding traffic impacts. TJKM's services to the
City of Dublin are pursuant to an agreement which provides for
compensation on an hourly basis.


To:
Re:
Date :
Page:
Kathy Donovan
Request for Advice:
October 9, L99O
2
covernment code S 83114 (b)
I am advised by I'lr. Springer that he will- not initially 
be
r,/orking on any projects for TJKM that involve the city of Dublin.
tte ind-icates LnLt 1t is tris understanding that every effort will
be made by TJKu not to assign hirn to $/ork on any project involving
the city of Dublin (including the area within its sphere of
influencL and extended planning area) but that it is possible that
such an assignrnent maY be made.
while TJKM will be a rrsource of income" to Mr. Springer, it
is unclear to us whether dj.squal j-f ication wilI be required. on the
one hand it would appear that Mr. Springer does not have a "finan-
cial interest'r within the meani,ng of Government Code SS 87100 and
871-03 by virtue of the provisions of Regulation 187.00.1(b) (4).
That Rdgulation appears to provide that an official 
is not
"atternptlng to use his or her official 
position to. influence a
governmentil decisiontr if the official 
prepares submissions of an
6ngineering nature rrto be used by a client in connection with a
pr6ceeding- before the agency.rr Hou/ever, this exception appears
iimea at the situation srhere an official 
is an engineer (or
ernployed by an engineering firm) preparing drawings or a submission
foi a- client, to be presented to the board on which the official
sits, rather than the situation here lthere the official-rs ernployer
prepares submissions for the city itself. 
Does this exception
ippiy in this situation? A second question arises regarding the
slcona sentence of subparagraph (4). If Mr. Springer receives the
agenda for the Planning Commission meeting which includes a traffic
r6port prepared by TJKI'I, can Mr. Springer contact the city p.lanning
stift 
witfr questions regarding the report without losing the
exception of subParagraph (4) ?
on the other hand, Mr. springer may be considered to have a
financial interest in a decision by virtue of the provisions of
Regulation t87o2.l, (a) (1) and (d). Subsection (a) (1) provides that
a person (including a business entity) that has been a source of
inlome to the official 
"is directly involved in a decision" if
there is a rrnexusrr between rrthe purpose for which the official
receives income and the governmental decision. t' 
Subsection (d)
def ines 'rnexusrr as being present 'rif the official- receives income
to achieve a goal or purpose which v'rould be achieved, defeated,
aided, or hindered by the decision. r' Again, it would appear that
this definition of rtnexusrr is geared more toward the situation
r^rhere an official, receives income from an applicant for land use
entittenent, rather than where the officialrs 
income is derived
(througrh his ernployer) fron the city for reports and analyses
prepared for purposes of evaluating applications.

Kathy Donovan
Re: 
Request for Advice: Government Code S 83114(b)
Dates 
october 9, 199 0

3
Thank you for your prompt response to this j,nquiry. Please
do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions or need
additional information.
Very truly yours,
MEYERS, NAVE, RTBACK & WEST

Re: Letter No. 90-644
Dear Ms. Bernardy:
To date I have not recei-ved a response to rny letter of October
9, requesting advice, recej-ved in your office on october l-1 , I99o.
Although I have telephoned you twice to find out the status of the
Ietter, I have not received a return telephone calL.
The City of Dublin requests a response to my letter on or
before December 3, 1990. There is at least one item scheduled for
the Planning Commission meeting of December 3, 1990, where the
Cityrs traffic 
engineer, TJKM, has performed work. As you will-
recaIl, one of the Commission members, CarI Springer, works for
TJKM. Due to one vacancy on the Commission and the anticipated
absence of one member on December 3 , L99O, there will only be three
members present, including Mr. Springer. There will thus not be
a quorum present to consider the item in question if Mr. Springer
has to disqualify himself. In fairness to the applicant, w€ would
request a response before December 3.
Very truly yours,
MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & WEST
